title: Freshly Fried Irish Potato Stir-fry
description: A fresh and fluffy festive dish featuring birds-eye chilies for a spicy kick, perfect for those looking for a quick dinner option.
instructions: Peel the potatoes, cut them into bite-sized pieces, and boil them until tender. Drain and set aside.
Dice the red bell pepper, onion, and garlic.
In a large skillet or wok, heat olive oil over medium-high heat. Add the diced vegetables (bell pepper, onion, garlic) and cook for about 5 minutes until softened.
Add the salted boiled potatoes to the skillet, tossing them around in the pan for even cooking.
When the veggies are well coated with oil and potatoes start to brown, add the frozen peas and lemon juice. Stir well to combine.
Increase heat to high and stir-fry for another 2-3 minutes until most of the liquid evaporates and vegetables are slightly crispy.
Add birds-eye chilies last and cook for an additional minute or two until they start to soften.
Adjust seasoning with more salt and pepper if needed, stirring to ensure everything is coated evenly.
